ops.title: Low findings from v0.12.1 watch-prefix review: docstring + direct-dep declarations
ops.description:
Two Low findings from the codex finder pass on c97d4f71 (v0.12.1), deferred because cc_steer/cli.py carried another session's uncommitted WIP at release time:

1. `pipeline_install_launchd`'s `--prefix` docstring/help (cc_steer/cli.py:~1075) documents only the retrain-extra rewrite; add the watch-extra rewrite (`uvx --from 'cc-steer[gate,mlx]' cc-steer` for the bare default) so operators supplying custom prefixes know the watch daemon needs gate+mlx.
2. Declare directly-imported packages as direct dependencies instead of relying on transitive metadata: `gate` extra imports scipy + joblib directly (cc_steer/watcher/gate.py:76-87, via scikit-learn today), `mlx` extra imports mlx.core directly (drafter_mlx.py:115, via mlx-lm today), and cc_steer/watcher/live.py:30 imports aiosqlite (supplied only via cc-transcript). Works in the current lock; fragile if upstreams reshuffle.

ops.text: install-launchd --prefix help mentions both prefix rewrites
scipy/joblib/mlx/aiosqlite declared where directly imported (or an explicit decision not to)
